behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because this behavior is rule evaluation logic (amount vs merchant-category threshold) within the scoring model. Pass 2 downgrade check: lower than Unit is not a valid level. Pass 2 upgrade check: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, real external API, or multi-module handoff; so Integration/System are not required. Rejection gate: rejected Integration+ because the behavior text is limited to computing the correct score for a threshold condition, not inter-module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring-rule function with in-memory config and a transaction above the category threshold; assert exact numeric score contribution and boundary behavior just below/above threshold.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because correctness depends on the risk model’s fraud-pattern prediction quality, not deterministic rule logic. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration with stubs would hide real model-quality failures (MOCK-HIDE). Pass 2 upgrade check: Workflow is not required since this behavior targets one model decision step, not multiple agentic steps in sequence. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes a single risk-model identification task.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluation against the real risk model on a labeled fraud-pattern fixture set where rule thresholds are not exceeded; assert fraud predictions/probabilities cross acceptance criteria for those cases.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because this is counting/filtering logic over timestamps per user in the scoring model. Pass 2 downgrade check: no lower level exists. Pass 2 upgrade check: behavior text does not require DB/filesystem/OS clock primitives or subprocesses; in-memory timestamps can expose off-by-one/window-boundary bugs. Rejection gate: rejected Integration+ because no explicit module-boundary failure is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke velocity-check logic with synthetic per-user transaction timestamps around window boundaries; assert exact counts (inclusive/exclusive edges) for configured windows.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure boundary is the handoff/combination of outputs from two model modules into hold/review decisioning (MOCK-MISS at module interaction). Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit could miss wiring errors if each model is correct but combiner logic or data mapping is wrong. Pass 2 upgrade check (Integration brake): no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess; no need for real model-quality dependence (model outputs can be controlled booleans/probabilities); no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic/System/Workflow because behavior text is purely OR-gate decision semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scoring-model adapter, risk-model adapter (stubbed outputs), and decision service together; feed case where risk=true and score=false and assert transaction status is held-for-review with correct reason flags.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is an output-contract aggregation across model outputs and review payload assembly. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit on a single formatter may miss real field-loss/mis-mapping between producer modules and aggregator. Pass 2 upgrade check (Integration brake): behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; does not require validating real AI quality, only presence/propagation of fields; not a multi-step agentic workflow. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic/System/Workflow due to explicit behavior focus on combined payload contents.
behaviors.test_description: Execute model-output aggregation path with representative scoring and risk outputs; assert final review payload contains numeric risk score, fraud probability, and per-model flag provenance fields.
